plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet the concentration mandate and reduce overall exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure, as per conservative mode.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to favor income and broad index exposure.
Buy VST at 5% as a top-quality new setup, adhering to single-name limits.
This plan aims for capital preservation by de-risking and focusing on stability.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall invested capital.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns, but also limited upside potential. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering overall exposure.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove leveraged ETFs and reduce overall leverage.
Increase QQQI to maintain some income exposure while managing risk.
Add to CRWD, a quality tech name with strong P&L, within limits.
Buy PLTR at 5% as a new strong tech setup from the scanner.
This plan balances risk and growth, aiming for a more diversified portfolio.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It aims for a blend of stability and growth, with moderate volatility and managed drawdowns. Leverage is reduced, but exposure to quality tech remains.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop, adhering to discipline.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press a strong performer, within limits.
Add to SMH, despite its downtrend, to maintain semiconductor exposure.
Buy VST at 10% as a high-scoring new setup from the scanner.
Buy KLAC at 10% as another high-scoring new setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It carries significantly higher risk and potential for larger drawdowns due to increased concentration and the use of leveraged ETFs. Daily decay and rebalancing of leveraged ETFs can erode returns quickly; strict stop discipline is paramount.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration mandate and reduce overall risk.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETFs.
Sell CRWD and SMH to reduce high-beta single name and sector exposure.
Significantly increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Increase QQQ to 20% for broad market exposure and stability.
This plan focuses on parking cash for yield and minimizing drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, prioritizing steady distributions and capital preservation. Expect lower growth potential but also significantly reduced volatility and drawdown risk. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns.
